title: Writable Layers, Volumes, Bind Mounts, tmpfs, Ownership, and Recovery · Docker Systems Brief: Docker Writable Layer Copy On Write
summary: A stateful service loses data after replacement, while a developer bind mount hides packaged files and writes host files as root. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker writable layer copy on write increment in storage selection, ownership, backup, and restore plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain immutable image layers, container writable-layer lifetime, copy-on-write costs, and containerd image-store versus classic-driver boundaries.
Set and verify CPU, memory, swap, PID, file, and device constraints and diagnose throttling, OOM, or unsupported host behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

steps.title: Predict Docker Writable Layer Copy On Write · Theory Docker Writable Layer Copy On Write
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory database writes into an anonymous volume while developers rely on a host-path bind mount. A backup archive exists but has never restored. Define ownership, consistency, lifecycle, checksum, and clean-volume recovery. Competency probe: Explain immutable image layers, container writable-layer lifetime, copy-on-write costs, and containerd image-store versus classic-driver boundaries.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Writable Layer Copy On Write contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example database writes into an anonymous volume while developers rely on a host-path bind mount. A backup archive exists but has never restored. Define ownership, consistency, lifecycle, checksum, and clean-volume recovery. Competency probe: Explain immutable image layers, container writable-layer lifetime, copy-on-write costs, and containerd image-store versus classic-driver boundaries. A defensible solution applies Docker Writable Layer Copy On Write, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to storage selection, ownership, backup, and restore plan.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-storage-mounts-backup-4
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-storage-mounts-backup-4
    artifact: storage-contract.yaml
    decision: worked docker storage mounts backup 4
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      mount-type: named-volume
      target: /var/lib/intake
      ownership: 10001:10001
      backup: application-consistent
      restore-test: fresh-volume
    verify: docker volume inspect intake-data && sha256sum restore-manifest.txt
    failure: Reject an anonymous volume as a named backup plan, bind-mount portability assumptions, or archive creation without a restore rehearsal.
    repair: Name ownership and lifecycle, quiesce or coordinate the application, checksum the artifact, and restore into a clean isolated volume.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The container writable layer is durable application storage that follows a replacement container. This fails the Docker Writable Layer Copy On Write contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
